title: Simplify V3CUse - do not consider implementations (.cpp) at all
body:
# Rationale
Currently Verilator includes _SYMS CLASS_ header to all implementations of classes. This header includes headers of all classes. This is far from a perfect solution. In fact it can severely slow down compilation of big projects as each new class adds up to the compilation time of all other classes. **However**, in order to solve this solution, we would likely have to do an overhaul of the way _SYMS CLASS_ works. It's almost guaranteed to appear as `vlSymsp` in nearly all implementations of classes in moderately complex projects. Until we can get rid of `vlSymsp`, or use it without including all those headers, there's no point in taking implementation requirements into consideration when adding `CUSE` nodes. If we figure out how to properly deal with dependencies in (.cpp) implementation files, we can always go back and reconsider the current logic used for adding `CUSE`s - if it remains adequate,

The current logic, however, prevent revising types which were already visited. Which meant than visiting a body of a function could result in skipping a CUSE for a type required in (.h) interface layer. We could just remove the logic that prevents DType revisits, but it turns out there isn't even a need to visit function bodies in the first place (aside from checking `return`s).
